hypothesis: If a 2B-parameter language model is continually pre-trained on MOF-focused literature and large chemical structure corpora before supervised fine-tuning on MOF property datasets using only MOFid text, then it will yield lower MAE than structure-based baselines and than the same model without such pre-training—especially with limited SFT data—because domain-specific pre-training imparts transferable chemical knowledge that enables accurate property prediction without 3D structures.
